title: Bayon - ANGKOR TEMPLES IN CAMBODIA
highlights: After Suryavarman's death (about 1150) there were factions fighting for power in the Khmer empire. The foreign Cham were involved, too, and in 1171 Cham invaders attacked Angkor. In 1177 or 1178 they seized the Khmer capital and could occupy it and could install Cham rulers for several years to come. In 1181 Jayavarman VII, who in the meantime was a kind of warlord collecting troops, managed to repulse them and to establish his own rule. He soon launched a massive building campaign.
